keyEvents.event: Declaration of independence and first Arab-Israeli war
keyEvents.impact: Born in war and surrounded by hostile neighbors; security became the organizing principle of the state
Preemptive victory captured Sinai, Golan, West Bank, and Gaza; cemented doctrine of striking first
Near-disaster seared intelligence vigilance and early-warning obsession into national doctrine

keyEvents.event: Camp David peace treaty with Egypt
keyEvents.impact: Removed the largest Arab army from the conflict in exchange for Sinai; land-for-peace precedent
Created the Palestinian Authority but collapsed into the Second Intifada, discrediting the peace camp
Normalization with the UAE and Bahrain reframed regional alignment against Iran

keyEvents.event: October 7 Hamas attack and Gaza war
keyEvents.impact: Worst single-day loss in state history shattered deterrence assumptions and ignited a multi-front war with Iran's proxies

history.narrative: A small state with existential memory, compensating for lack of strategic depth with a qualitative military edge, world-class intelligence, and a doctrine of preemption. Backed by an ironclad American alliance and an undeclared nuclear arsenal, it treats an Iranian bomb as a threat it cannot live with.

redLines.description: Iranian acquisition of a nuclear weapon
Large-scale attack on Israeli population centers or existential military threat
Rupture of the American security relationship and qualitative military edge

internalDivisions.name: Secular-Haredi divide over conscription and the character of the state
Judicial overhaul crisis and left-right polarization
Arab citizens of Israel and the occupied territories conflict

goals.description: Prevent Iran from acquiring a nuclear weapon, by force if necessary
Maintain the qualitative military edge over all regional adversaries
Dismantle Iranian proxy threats on the northern and southern borders
Normalize relations with Saudi Arabia and expand the Abraham Accords
